frequency_mhz.source: OpenTitan EarlGrey: 100 MHz achieved on sky130A with Ibex (same core, same process). NEORV32: 50 MHz on FPGA, ~100-200 MHz on 130nm ASIC estimated. Target 50 MHz is midpoint between conservative (25 MHz: low-effort, basically guaranteed) and ambitious (100 MHz: OpenTitan parity, requires careful STA).

power_active_mw.source: TI MSP430FR2355: 2.9 mW active @ 24 MHz, 130nm FRAM. STM32L011D4: 0.28 mW @ 32 MHz, 110nm ULP. Silabs EFM8BB10: 3.75 mW @ 25 MHz, 180nm scaled to sky130A ~2.6 mW. Ibex at 50 MHz sky130A estimated 2-5 mW (core only), full SoC 5-10 mW. Conservative 10 mW assumes no clock gating; ambitious 3 mW assumes aggressive clock gating + low-Vt cells.

sleep_power_uw.source: TI MSP430FR2355: 0.5 µA standby with RAM retention at 3V = ~1.5 µW. STM32L011D4: 0.5 µA stop mode = ~0.9 µW at 1.8V. Sky130A SRAM retention at 50 MHz domain typically 5-50 µW range. Conservative 50 µW assumes entire SoC clock-gated but not power-gated; ambitious 5 µW assumes power gating with state retention.

area_mm2.source: OpenTitan EarlGrey: ~1.0 mm² core area on sky130A (Ibex + 128KB SRAM + 20+ peripherals). Our design: Ibex (~25 kGE) + 2-4KB OpenRAM SRAM (~30-60 kGE) + 6 peripherals (~8 kGE) + APB bus + Caravel wrapper (~8 kGE) = ~70-100 kGE total. At sky130A std cell density ~3.5 µm²/GE with 60% utilization, physical area ≈ 0.4-0.6 mm² (ambitious) to 1.5-2.0 mm² (conservative, includes padding and test structures). Caravel user area is 2,800×1,760µm = 4.93 mm² — all targets fit comfortably.

ip_reuse_ratio.source: OpenTitan: ~90% REUSE (all comportable IP). IP-INDEX: fossi-ef-uart, fossi-ef-spi, fossi-ef-i2c, fossi-ef-gpio8, fossi-ef-tmr32, lowrisc-ibex, vlsida-openram — all STRONG tier, Apache-2.0 or BSD-3-Clause. 6/6 core peripherals available as STRONG REUSE IP. Caravel wrapper + APB bridge are CREATE. Expected REUSE ratio: 80-85%.

must_have: SPI master interface (mode 0/3, up to 12.5 MHz)
Caravel Wishbone bridge and harness integration
differentiators: Fully open-source (Apache-2.0 / BSD-3-Clause) — no proprietary IP
STRONG-tier REUSE for all core peripherals (EF_ IP family + Ibex + OpenRAM)
Audited integration with formal verification per module
Reference design for open-source environmental sensing
Publish sleep/active power measurements on sky130A — no prior open-source data
exclusions: Analog front-end (ADC, op-amps, comparators) — digital-only SoC
Wireless (BLE, Zigbee, LoRa) — external module expected
Hardware crypto (AES, SHA, TRNG) — out of scope for sensor hub
Floating-point unit — RV32I only, no F extension
Cache — direct SRAM access, no cache hierarchy
Boot ROM — firmware loaded via Caravel management SoC or external SPI flash

caravel_area_budget_mm2.note: Caravel user area = 2,800 × 1,760 µm. Conservative allocation uses <41% of available area — ample room for routing, test structures, and future expansion.
